definition.name: Weis_Shirokov_theorem
definition.definition: (Weis-Shirokov, 2021, Theorem 2). Let K be a convex set in a locally convex space and f : K → R ∪ {+∞} a lower semicontinuous generalized affine map. Let C be a sublevel set C = {x ∈ K : f(x) ≤ β} or a level set C = {x ∈ K : f(x) = β}. For any point x ∈ C: dim(F_K(x)) ≤ dim(F_C(x)) + 1. More generally (Corollary 5), under ℓ generalized affine constraints (i.e., C is the intersection of ℓ sublevel/level sets): dim(F_K(x)) ≤ dim(F_C(x)) + ℓ. In particular, if x is extreme in C (dim(F_C(x)) = 0), then dim(F_K(x)) ≤ ℓ. This is the abstract convex-geometric core of both Carathéodory-type and Barvinok-Pataki-type results. Reference: Weis-Shirokov, J. Convex Anal. 28(3), 847-870, 2021, arXiv:2003.14302.
